translation_findings.jp: エ．男性 OR (20歳未満 OR 65歳以上)
translation_findings.zh: D. 男性 OR （20岁以下 OR 65岁及以上）
translation_findings.en: D. Male OR (Under 20 OR 65 and over)
translation_findings.issue: Internal inconsistency: choices A/B/C render '20歳未満' as '未满20岁' and '65歳以上' as '65岁以上', but choice D uses '20岁以下' (which in zh can include 20, ambiguous) and '65岁及以上'. Also extra fullwidth space before opening paren.
translation_findings.suggested_fix: D. 男性 OR（未满20岁 OR 65岁以上）

translation_findings.jp: エ．男性 OR (20歳未満 OR 65歳以上)
translation_findings.zh: D. 男性 OR （20岁以下 OR 65岁及以上）
translation_findings.en: D. Male OR (Under 20 OR 65 and over)
translation_findings.issue: Choices A/B/C use '65 or older'; choice D uses '65 and over' — minor inconsistency.
translation_findings.suggested_fix: D. Male OR (Under 20 OR 65 or older)

translation_findings.jp: 二つの集合AとBについて、常に成立する関係を記述したものはどれか。ここで、(X∩Y)は、XとYの共通部分（積集合）、(X∪Y)は、X又はYの少なくとも一方に属する部分（和集合）を表す。
translation_findings.zh: 关于两个集合A和B，描述始终成立的关系的是哪一项？此处，A与B的交集表示X与Y的公共部分（积集合），A与B的并集表示属于X或Y中至少一方的部分（和集合）。
translation_findings.en: Which of the following describes a relationship that always holds for two sets A and B? Here, (X ∩ Y) represents the common part of X and Y (intersection), and (X ∪ Y) represents the part belonging to at least one of X or Y (union).
translation_findings.issue: zh stem mis-renders the notation definition: jp defines (X∩Y) and (X∪Y) using generic placeholders X/Y, but zh replaces them with 'A与B的交集' and 'A与B的并集', conflating the question's actual sets (A,B) with the notation definition. Meaning is recoverable from context, so not release-blocking, but the symbol legend is corrupted.
translation_findings.suggested_fix: 关于两个集合A和B，描述始终成立的关系的是哪一项？此处，(X∩Y) 表示X与Y的公共部分（积集合），(X∪Y) 表示属于X或Y中至少一方的部分（和集合）。

translation_findings.jp: ウ．(A∪B)は、(A∩B)の部分集合である。
translation_findings.zh: C. A与B的并集是A与B的交集的子集。
translation_findings.en: C. A ∪ B (Union) is a subset of A ∩ B (Intersection).
translation_findings.issue: Adds parenthetical glosses '(Union)' and '(Intersection)' not in jp; harmless but inconsistent with sibling choices (A,B,D) which use only the symbols.
translation_findings.suggested_fix: C. (A ∪ B) is a subset of (A ∩ B).

narrative: 3 questions; semantics OK overall. Q1 choice D shows internal terminology inconsistency in zh/en (MED/LOW polish, non-blocking). Q2 stem zh substitutes the symbol-definition placeholders X/Y with A/B (MED, semantically recoverable). Choice markers ア/イ/ウ/エ -> A/B/C/D correctly applied throughout.
